content.claim: Early tracking correlates with increased educational inequality measured by outcome dispersion, and cross-national evidence suggests early tracking reduces mean performance, though this causal effect is contested in some methodological approaches.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: The CEU thesis notes that long-term tertiary education effects could not be conclusively established
One study reports zero tertiary education effect; this finding is not widely replicated in the notes
Causal attribution is challenged by multiple simultaneous selection and policy changes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
